title: Family Communication Patterns and Resilience
description: Family resilience in long-distance marriages is shaped by how families communicate and manage work-family conflict. Effective family communication patterns and constructive handling of work-family conflict both play significant roles in supporting resilience among families experiencing long-distance marriage.
body:
Open and supportive communication within families is directly linked to higher resilience, helping families adapt to adversity and maintain satisfaction despite physical separation.
Patterns emphasizing dialogue (open discussion, sharing feelings) are especially predictive of resilience, while conformity-oriented patterns (emphasizing agreement and hierarchy) have a less direct but still significant effect.

In long-distance marriages, protective factors such as commitment, effective communication, openness, and collaborative conflict resolution are crucial for maintaining marital satisfaction and resilience.
